# Copilot instructions for this repository

Purpose
- Provide concise, repo-specific instructions to help Copilot sessions reason about build/test/lint commands, architecture, and local conventions.

Quick commands
- Server (from /server):
  - Install: npm install
  - Dev / run: npm run dev      (nodemon + ts-node)
  - Start (production-like): npm start (runs ts-node src/index.ts)
  - Build: npm run build       (tsc)
- Client (from /client):
  - Install: npm install
  - Start dev server: npm start
  - Build: npm run build
  - Test (full): npm test
  - Run a single test by name: npm test -- -t "<test name>"  (react-scripts supports -t)
- Seed static JSON data (from repo root):
  - npx ts-node scripts/seed.ts

Notes: there are no repo-wide lint or test runner config files in this repo root; rely on per-package scripts if/when added.

High-level architecture (big picture)
- Monorepo-style layout with three main packages/folders:
  - server: Express + TypeScript backend. Entry: server/src/index.ts. Routes are mounted under /api (see server/src/routes/api.ts). Business logic lives in services/ (e.g., matchSimulation). Models are simple TypeScript classes in server/src/models.
  - client: React + TypeScript frontend (create-react-app/react-scripts). Pages/components under client/src/pages and client/src/components. App entry: client/src/index.tsx.
  - shared: canonical TypeScript types/interfaces used by both client and server (shared/src/types). Prefer updating types here for cross-cutting changes.
- Data seeding is done by scripts/seed.ts which writes JSON to a local data/ directory used for simple local state.

Key conventions and patterns (repo-specific)
- TypeScript everywhere: models are classes (server/src/models/*) and shared types are the contract between client/server.
- Routes/controllers: routes under server/src/routes mount controller methods that are bound (e.g., controller.method.bind(controller)). Treat controllers as stateful classes.
- API base path: /api. Expect REST-style endpoints like /api/teams.
- Simulation code (pure-ish functions): services/ contains deterministic/semi-random domain logic (e.g., matchSimulation). Keep simulation logic isolated from route handlers.
- Dev commands are package-scoped: run server commands from the server folder and client commands from the client folder.
- No global DB or ORM: data seeding and storage are file-based JSON for local dev (scripts/seed.ts). If adding a DB, update seed and controllers accordingly.

Files and places to check for changes
- Update shared/src/types when changing payload shapes used across client/server.
- Add tests by adding scripts in package.json for the package (client uses react-scripts test — supports single-test filter with -t).
- If introducing linters or CI, add package.json scripts and a root-level workflow under .github/workflows.

What Copilot should assume
- Default ports: client dev at 3000 (CRA default), server at 3000 (server uses process.env.PORT || 3000). When both run concurrently, environment adjustments are needed.
- npm is the package manager used. Use npx when executing TypeScript node scripts from the repo root.
- There are currently no automated tests or linters configured in the server package by default; suggest adding them if requested.

When editing code
- Update shared types first for breaking changes, then update client and server code to match.
- Keep simulation/business logic in services/* to keep routes/controllers thin.

Other AI/assistant configs
- No CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, AGENTS.md, or AIDER/CONVENTIONS files present. If one is added, merge relevant guidance into this file.

If you want: configure MCP servers (e.g., Playwright for end-to-end tests or a headless browser environment) to run browser tests against the client dev server. Ask if you want that configured and which test runner to use.

---
